reproducible_artifact: Per-engineer attention-architecture configuration, version-controlled, with named restraint windows and per-channel SNR audits. Concrete artifact: a `attention-config.yaml` file in the engineer's dotfiles repo that controls notification routing per work block; coupled to a deep-work calendar block that automatically applies the configuration on entry.
embodiment_sketch:
An engineer's `attention-config.yaml` looks like:
```yaml
blocks:
  deep_architectural:
    schedule: 'Tue/Thu 09:00-13:00'
    pagerduty: services_owned_only severity_above_warning
    slack: dnd channels_blocked: [all]
    linear: digest 12:00
    github: mentions_batched_email
    twitter: dns_block
    sentry: noisy_channels_muted
  oncall:
    pagerduty: all
    slack: priority_only
    linear: realtime
```
A `enter-deep-work` script reads the config and applies it via vendor APIs (Slack DND API, PagerDuty schedule overrides, hosts file edits); an `exit-deep-work` script restores. Falsification: across two months of A/B (configured deep-work blocks vs unconfigured blocks of equal scheduled length), measure (a) reviewer-discovered defect rate post-block, (b) the engineer's self-reported state-loaded percentage of block time. The verse predicts both metrics improve under configured blocks. The team can audit the configuration document and the enter/exit script — these are reviewable artifacts. Pass.
